Synthesized valuation/quality/momentum snapshot for each initial analyst pick (BJ, KR, COST, WBA, UNFI) based on tool-generated market news, price momentum, and qualitative/quantitative risk factors. Key evidence includes: 1) BJ: strong long-term momentum, recent negative technicals, moderate valuation; 2) KR: solid long-term/short-term performance, reasonable valuation, private label leadership; 3) COST: high quality, premium valuation, negative short-term momentum; 4) WBA: event-driven PE buyout, structurally impaired, negative ROE, major liabilities; 5) UNFI: positive recent price trend, but margin compression and high leverage persist. Evidence now available for factor tilt and portfolio construction steps.

BJ: Valuation moderate (P/E ~22), technicals bearish short-term (death cross, 8% 1-month drop), long-term positive (27% 12m gain), fundamental quality strong (membership/digital growth, EPS guide raised), but recent insider selling and cautious analyst tone.

KR: Reasonable valuation (P/E ~14-16), strong LT momentum (119% 5y, 37% 12m), moderate 1m decline, operational excellence (private label strength, margin focus), but leadership transition risk and Walmart/Amazon competitive pressures.

COST: Premium valuation (P/E >50), highest operational quality (membership, earnings, scale), but negative short-term momentum (death cross, 7/10 days down, technical breakdown), insider selling, and analyst downgrades on price risk.

WBA: Event-driven PE buyout at $11.45/share, high dividend yield (~8%), negative ROE (–69%), major opioid liabilities, healthcare underperformance (VillageMD writedown), but strong cashflow; near-term path dictated by deal closure, not fundamentals.

UNFI: +50% 12m price momentum, but viewed as relief rally, not fundamental improvement. Margin compression (gross margin ~13%), high net debt ($2B), operational recovery post-cyberattack, labor deals secure, analysts caution on "value trap" until margins/debt improve.

Call episodic_recall to verify retrieval of 'portfolio_v1'

tasks.description: Analyze Portfolio V1 risks, exposures, and performance

subtasks.description: Call exposure_calculator for net, gross, long, short exposures
Call correlation_matrix to identify high-correlation pairs (>0.5)
Call calculate_portfolio_beta_vs_index vs 'SPY' for market beta
Call factor_tilts_for_portfolio with factors='all' for style exposures
Call industry_concentration at 'industry' and 'sub_industry' levels
Call VaR_calculator at 'portfolio' and 'industry' levels
Call calculate_portfolio_past_performance for 3Y risk/return metrics
Synthesize strengths/weaknesses and changes needed (no tool)

tasks.description: Create Portfolio V2 and re-run analytics

subtasks.description: Adjust names/weights to reduce beta/correlation and improve factor/valuation fit (no tool)
Call episodic_remember to store 'portfolio_v2' with deltas vs V1
Call episodic_recall to verify 'portfolio_v2'
Repeat 4a–4g analytics on V2 using same tools
Document remaining gaps; decide if further iteration needed (no tool)

tasks.description: Create Portfolio V3 (and iterate further if needed) and re-run analytics

subtasks.description: Refine positions/weights to meet hard constraints and targets (no tool)
Call episodic_remember to store 'portfolio_v3' with rationale
Call episodic_recall to verify 'portfolio_v3'
Repeat 4a–4g analytics on V3 using same tools
Call get_completion_analysis or check_plan_completion_status to confirm readiness; iterate more if not ready
Lock final tickers and allocations satisfying 15-20 longs, 10-15 shorts, net ~+30%, gross ≤200% (no tool)
Call build_portfolio with final portfolio_dict to optimize and size positions
Validate with exposure_calculator, calculate_portfolio_beta_vs_index, and correlation_matrix; adjust if needed
Draft thesis and key drivers per position using collected evidence (no tool, no hallucination)
Call episodic_remember to store 'final_portfolio' and decision rationale
Call add_task_evidence to attach key analytics, charts, and summaries
Call update_task_status and mark_task_complete as tasks/subtasks finish
Call get_execution_summary and get_plan_analytics_report for final review

tasks.description: Formatting and delivery of final answer (must be last)

subtasks.description: Format final output exactly per user JSON schema: array of {ticker, position, thesis, key_drivers, allocation} (no tool)
Ensure tool input dictionary formatting rules were followed and no extra text in final output (no tool)
